role_title: Program Manager, Customer Stories & Advocacy, NYC

level_signals: 5–8 years of experience in customer marketing, storytelling, advocacy, program management, or related roles
Compensation range $116,200–$174,200 consistent with mid-senior IC
Role owns strategy, roadmap, and execution of a cross-functional program

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 5–8 years of experience in customer marketing, storytelling, advocacy, program management, or related roles at a high-growth SaaS or technology company
must_haves.qualifier: at a high-growth SaaS or technology company

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Proven experience owning end-to-end customer stories or advocacy programs, from strategy and writing through execution
Exceptional writing, editing, and storytelling skills, with a proven ability to independently produce polished, executive-ready customer stories and advocacy content
Meaningful experience with video production—including planning shoots, working with production crews on site, managing post-production workflows, and delivering polished video content
Strong program management skills with exceptional attention to detail and follow-through
Excellent written and verbal communication skills; comfortable engaging with senior executives and internal stakeholders

scope.surface: Customer Stories & Advocacy program — written and video customer stories, Innovation Spotlight program, executive advocacy engine
scope.business_model: B2B enterprise SaaS — legal and professional services AI platform
primary_metrics_inferred: Volume and quality of published customer stories
GTM and product launch enablement via customer content
Executive-level credibility and advocacy reach (Innovation Spotlight)
Operational efficiency of storytelling and advocacy workflows
core_responsibilities: Own the strategy, roadmap, and execution of Harvey's Customer Stories program
Collaborate with internal creative teams and external production partners to plan, produce, and deliver video customer stories
Scale and operationalize the Innovation Spotlight program spotlighting senior customer leaders
Build operational backbone for customer stories and advocacy programs including intake processes, editorial calendars, production workflows, approval processes, and localization frameworks
Partner cross-functionally with Product Marketing, Sales, and Customer Success to align stories with core narratives, use cases, and launches
Build trusted, long-term relationships with senior customer stakeholders

green_flags: Clear program ownership and scope — not a vague role
High-visibility cross-functional role with executive exposure
Company has strong product-market fit signal (1000+ customers in 60+ countries)
Defined output areas: written stories, video stories, Innovation Spotlight
red_flags: No mention of visa sponsorship or work authorization — unclear policy
No mention of equity despite being a high-growth startup — notable omission
Role requires NYC presence (title includes 'NYC') but JD body does not explicitly state hybrid vs. full on-site expectations
No nice-to-haves listed — JD is thin on differentiation between must-haves and preferences

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from years required + comp range)
location.type set to 'onsite' inferred from 'NYC' in role title; JD body does not specify hybrid or remote policy
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (not stated in JD; inferred from responsibilities)
equity_mentioned: false (not stated; notable omission for a startup)
ambiguities: Location type: 'NYC' in role title implies on-site but JD body never explicitly states remote/hybrid/on-site policy — treated as onsite here
Visa sponsorship and work authorization policy not mentioned anywhere in the JD — marked as 'unclear'
No nice-to-haves section present in JD; all listed qualifications treated as must-haves under 'What You Have'
